title: Spring Boot 4 中 Kotlin 支持的提升
author: sdeleuze
content:
Following the announcement of the strategic partnership between JetBrains and Spring in May, I would like to share a global update on various Kotlin-related features and documentation enhancements we have made recently, with the goal of making Spring Boot 4 the best framework to develop backend applications with Kotlin.

Kotlin 2 baseline

Kotlin 2.0 introduced a new stable K2 compiler that compiles projects faster, performs smarter code analysis and allows the Kotlin team to evolve the language faster than with the K1 compiler. We recommend IntelliJ IDEA users to upgrade to 2025.3 to benefit from the best developer experience.

Spring Framework 7.0, Spring Boot 4.0 and the rest of the portfolio switches from Kotlin 1 to Kotlin 2 with a new Kotlin 2.2 baseline that we chose for multiple reasons:

  • Automatic translation of JSpecify to Kotlin null-safety is only enabled by default as of Kotlin 2.1
  • A Kotlin developer experience as consistent as possible (with defaults when no use-site targets are specified for example)
  • Limit the maintenance overhead for the Spring team

The policy we plan for future version is consistent with what has been done so far:

  • Spring Boot 4.1 should leverage Kotlin 2.3 which adds Java 25 support while retaining a Kotlin 2.2 baseline
  • Spring Framework 7.1, Spring Boot 4.2 and related portfolio projects should leverage Kotlin 2.4 while also retaining a Kotlin 2.2 baseline

The recommendation for libraries depending on Spring Framework 7 is to define the same baseline with Kotlin compiler options apiVersion=2.2 and languageVersion=2.2 to not accidentally break the compatibility when upgrading the Kotlin version.

We also recommend defining a Kotlin baseline for new versions of libraries supporting Kotlin typically used in Spring Boot 4 applications, but not depending on Spring. In that case, the baseline can typically be more lenient with for example apiVersion=2.0 and languageVersion=2.0, if you don’t leverage JSpecify annotations, don’t have to maintain a compiler plugin and if the version of the Kotlin compiler you are using supports this baseline.

Idiomatic null-safe APIs with JSpecify

I had the opportunity to describe recently how JSpecify usage across the Spring portfolio enables writing null-safe Java applications with Spring Boot 4, but it also turns Spring APIs to idiomatic null-safe Kotlin ones.

Spring Framework 5 and 6 provided some initial null-safety support, but this new major version brings it to a whole new level, since unlike the previous JSR 305 arrangement, JSpecify annotations:

  • Allow to specify the nullability of generic types and array elements
  • Are applied to the whole Spring portfolio, not just Spring Framework and Spring Data
  • Are automatically translated to Kotlin nullability
  • Are checked when building Spring portfolio projects with NullAway

When upgrading to Spring Boot 4, you should see no unsafe platform types in Spring, Reactor or Micrometer APIs anymore, just Kotlin null-safe types. That may require some slight refinements when migrating due to some nullability changes, but hopefully nothing too painful.

Better Kotlin Serialization support

A more predictable behavior

Over the years, the combined usage of Kotlin Serialization alongside another general purpose JSON library like Jackson has triggered the creation of a lot of bug reports. One of the reasons is that Kotlin Serialization is not a general purpose JSON serialization library, it is designed to handle mostly Kotlin classes annotated with @Serializable, but also some other cases like:

  • Unannotated Kotlin enums
  • Containers shared between Java and Kotlin like List, Map, etc.
  • Numbers, characters, booleans and strings

That means there is a grey area where it is not always obvious to determine what should be handled by Kotlin Serialization and what should be handled by Jackson.

Also, it is important to keep in mind that even in Spring Boot applications using Kotlin Serialization, Jackson remains needed for features like actuators. So the combined usage of both Kotlin Serialization and Jackson is very common.

In order to solve this recurring problem, we have decided to make the Spring support for Kotlin Serialization more predictable. With Spring Boot 4.0, when both Kotlin Serialization and Jackson are used, the latter will handle only types annotated with @Serializable at root or generic type level (see related utility method). When only Kotlin Serialization is used, it will continue to handle all the types supported by the library.

If you need to customize the behavior deciding which types Kotlin Serialization should handle, you can do it easily via the new org.springframework.http.converter.HttpMessageConverters type crafted by Brian Clozel. See for example how to configure it in Spring Boot with Spring MVC. New constructors in related converters and codecs allowing to customize that behavior with a predicate are available.

A new Spring Boot dedicated module

Spring Boot 4 now provides a new spring-boot-kotlinx-serialization-json module, with a related spring-boot-starter-kotlinx-serialization-json starter (renamed in Spring Boot 4.0.1). Just add one of those dependencies, as well as the related compiler plugin, and you are done.

DSL-based registration of Spring beans

BeanRegistrar and BeanRegistrarDsl provide flexible bean registration capabilities, allowing for example custom registration through an if expression or a for loop, something that is not possible with @Bean.

Their hidden superpower is that they allow creating custom configuration DSLs for Spring Boot applications, in the spirit of Spring Fu that we recently retired, with the advantage of being fully integrated with Spring Boot 4 and its new modular design. KHTMX for Spring is a great example of that. Don't be shy, write your own in Java or Kotlin!

In your Kotlin application, feel free to choose between @Bean and BeanRegistrarDsl for declaring your beans, after all it is also a matter of style preference. Check Spring PetKlinik, my Kotlin fullstack version of the famous Spring Petclinic sample application, if you want to see how such programmatic bean registration looks like in practice combined with the router DSL.

Automatic context propagation with Coroutines

Spring Boot 4.0 and Spring Framework 7.0 introduces automatic context propagation with Coroutines, allowing observability and tracing to work in suspending functions. To enable it, just define a spring.reactor.context-propagation=auto Spring Boot property, and make sure the io.micrometer:context-propagation dependency is present.

Most popular Spring guides available in Kotlin

Previously only available in Java, the most popular Spring guides are now also available in Kotlin thanks to the contributions of Danil Pavlov from the Kotlin team, and have been upgraded to Spring Boot 4:

  • Building a RESTful Web Service
  • Building a Reactive RESTful Web Service
  • Consuming a RESTful Web Service
  • Building an Application with Spring Boot
  • Uploading Files
  • Securing a Web Application
  • Enabling Cross Origin Requests for a RESTful Web Service
  • Accessing Relational Data using JDBC with Spring
  • Accessing Data with JPA
  • Managing Transactions
  • Testing the Web Layer

The official Spring Boot Kotlin tutorial has also been migrated from Spring Data JPA to Spring Data JDBC, which allows more idiomatic Kotlin code and a more lightweight stack. It has also been upgraded to Spring Boot 4 and modernized.

What’s next?

The 2026 roadmap is not yet fully defined, but I intend to explore the following areas that may or may not materialize in our releases:

  • Leverage the more efficient kotlin-reflect based on kotlin-metadata-jvm
  • Better efficiency and performance when using Kotlin inline value classes
  • Fullstack rendering with Compose HTML and Kotlin/Wasm
  • Idiomatic logging in Kotlin
  • Enhanced support for Coroutines
    • Native support in Spring MVC (without the reactive bridge)
    • Virtual Thread dispatcher
  • Even more Kotlin-friendly Spring Framework reference documentation
  • Declarative build systems (Declarative Gradle, Amper)

Feel free to share your ideas, asks and hopes!
aiSummary: 本文详细介绍了 Spring Boot 4 增强的 Kotlin 支持，包括 K2 编译器基线、通过 JSpecify 改进的空值安全性、可预测的 Kotlin 序列化、基于 DSL 的 bean 注册，以及自动上下文传播等。
oneSentenceSummary: 本文详细介绍了 Spring Boot 4 增强的 Kotlin 支持，包括 K2 编译器基线、通过 JSpecify 改进的空值安全性、可预测的 Kotlin 序列化、基于 DSL 的 bean 注册，以及自动上下文传播等。
summary: 本文宣布了 Spring Boot 4 在 Kotlin 支持方面取得的重大进展，旨在将其打造成为使用 Kotlin 进行后端开发的卓越框架。关键更新包括采用 Kotlin 2 和 K2 编译器作为基线，这带来了更快的编译速度和更智能的代码分析。一个主要亮点是通过 Spring 所有系列产品中的 JSpecify 注解增强了空值安全性，提供了符合 Kotlin 习惯的空值安全 API 和强大的空值检查。Kotlin 序列化支持变得更具可预测性，解决了之前与 Jackson 的集成问题，并且新增了一个专用的 `spring-boot-kotlinx-serialization-json` 模块，简化了设置。此外，Spring Boot 4 引入了基于 DSL 的 bean 注册以实现灵活配置，并为 Coroutines（协程）提供了自动上下文传播，从而在挂起函数中实现了更好的可观测性和追踪。最后，流行的 Spring 指南已更新并翻译成 Kotlin，官方的 Spring Boot Kotlin 教程现在使用 Spring Data JDBC 来生成更符合习惯的代码。文章还概述了未来的路线图项目，例如利用 `kotlin-reflect` 和 `kotlin-inline-value-classes`、使用 Compose HTML/Kotlin/Wasm 进行全栈渲染以及增强的 Coroutines 支持。

mainPoints.point: Spring Boot 4 采用 Kotlin 2 和 K2 编译器作为其基线，以提高性能和语言演进。
mainPoints.explanation: 转向 Kotlin 2.2 基线可确保更快的编译速度、更智能的代码分析，并与自动 JSpecify 空值安全转换保持一致，从而降低了 Spring 团队的维护开销。

mainPoints.point: JSpecify 注解为所有 Spring 项目带来了全面的符合习惯的空值安全 API。
mainPoints.explanation: 这种新的空值安全处理方法支持泛型类型和数组元素，自动转换为 Kotlin 空值安全类型，并使用 NullAway 进行检查，显著增强了 API 的健壮性。

mainPoints.point: Kotlin 序列化集成通过新的专用 Spring Boot 模块变得更具可预测性和流线型。
mainPoints.explanation: 为了解决过去的问题，Spring Boot 4 明确了 Kotlin 序列化与 Jackson 的交互方式，并提供了一个新的 `spring-boot-kotlinx-serialization-json` 模块，以便更轻松地设置和使用。

mainPoints.point: 基于 DSL 的 bean 注册提供了灵活且可定制的 Spring bean 配置。
mainPoints.explanation: BeanRegistrar 和 BeanRegistrarDsl 允许使用条件逻辑进行程序化 bean 注册，支持自定义配置 DSL，并为开发人员提供了 `@Bean` 的替代方案。

mainPoints.point: Coroutines 的自动上下文传播增强了挂起函数中的可观测性与追踪。
mainPoints.explanation: 通过启用 `spring.reactor.context-propagation=auto` 并添加 `io.micrometer:context-propagation`，开发人员可以确保可观测性与追踪在 Coroutines 中无缝工作。

keyQuotes: 继 JetBrains 和 Spring 在 5 月宣布战略合作之后，我想分享我们最近在各种 Kotlin 相关特性和文档增强方面取得的全球性更新，旨在使 Spring Boot 4 成为使用 Kotlin 开发后端应用程序的最佳框架。
升级到 Spring Boot 4 后，您将不再看到 Spring、Reactor 或 Micrometer API 中不安全的平台类型，而是仅有 Kotlin 空值安全类型。
使用 Spring Boot 4.0 时，当同时使用 Kotlin 序列化和 Jackson 时，后者将仅处理根级别或泛型类型级别上带有 `@Serializable` 注解的类型。
BeanRegistrar 和 BeanRegistrarDsl 提供了灵活的 bean 注册功能，例如允许通过 `if` 表达式或 `for` 循环进行自定义注册，这在 `@Bean` 中是不可能实现的。
Spring Boot 4.0 和 Spring Framework 7.0 引入了 Coroutines 的自动上下文传播，允许可观测性与追踪在挂起函数中工作。
